hero:
# WESAD Stress Detection

**Wearable physiological stress-state classification · Python · TensorFlow · Scikit-learn**

## Hero Overview

A wearable-sensor pipeline classifying stress vs. non-stress states from multi-channel Empatica E4 streams (BVP, HR, EDA, TEMP, ACC) across 15 participants, framed as a daily-wearable monitoring concept.

Sequence models (Bi-LSTM, CNN-LSTM) trained with subject-level cross-validation translate raw physiological signal into interpretable classification outputs.

subsystems: Empatica E4 streams (TEMP, EDA, BVP, HR, ACC)
points.evidence: Ingested multi-channel Empatica E4 sensor streams across 15 participants, resampling signals to aligned frequencies.
Engineered 30+ temporal and statistical features per channel, applying PCA (~40% reduction) and Z-score normalization.
Trained Bi-LSTM and CNN-LSTM time-series classifiers reaching 0.81 F1 (~79% accuracy) on stress-state classification.
Leave-One-Group-Out cross-validation across 15 participants to test subject-level, unseen-user generalization.

network_graph.title: Technology Capability Network
network_graph.description: Maps the project's core techniques to the tools used to implement them.

storyline:
# Storyline

## Context

Acute-stress episodes are hard to catch in the moment; wearable physiological signal offers a continuous, non-invasive monitoring lens if it can be modeled reliably across people.

## Workflow

Multi-channel sensor ingestion → per-channel feature engineering → dimensionality reduction → sequence modeling → subject-level validation → interpretable classification output.

## Technical Implementation

- **Ingestion:** Empatica E4 streams (BVP, HR, EDA, TEMP, ACC) across 15 participants, resampled to aligned frequencies.
- **Features:** 30+ temporal/statistical features per channel, PCA (~40% reduction) and Z-score normalization for stable inputs.
- **Modeling:** Bi-LSTM and CNN-LSTM classifiers (TensorFlow + Scikit-learn), 0.81 F1 (~79% accuracy).
- **Validation:** Leave-One-Group-Out CV across 15 participants simulating unseen-user generalization.

## Outcomes

Framed sensor-driven classification as a daily-wearable monitoring concept, translating model outputs into interpretable classification outputs for downstream review.
